notes: Trade-risk screening preserves jurisdiction separation and does not borrow MRLs or reference values across authorities.

A pass status only means all applicable governed limits that were actually found were met for that jurisdiction; inspect MRL and reference-value coverage statuses before treating the lane as complete.
Inconclusive jurisdictions remain active review signals, not implicit passes: CN, CODEX_GLOBAL, US.

review_prompts.prompt: Treat the US lane as review-required until the missing or partial MRL coverage is explicitly accepted.
Use `mrlCoverageStatus` for US to distinguish partial curated scope, anchor-only posture, and explicit gaps instead of flattening them into a generic missing-limit state.
Use `referenceValueJurisdictionStatus` for US to confirm whether the reference-value side is exact, anchor-only, family-curated-without-value, or an explicit gap.
Treat the CODEX_GLOBAL lane as review-required until the missing or partial MRL coverage is explicitly accepted.
Use `mrlCoverageStatus` for CODEX_GLOBAL to distinguish partial curated scope, anchor-only posture, and explicit gaps instead of flattening them into a generic missing-limit state.
Treat the CN lane as review-required until the missing or partial MRL coverage is explicitly accepted.
Use `mrlCoverageStatus` for CN to distinguish partial curated scope, anchor-only posture, and explicit gaps instead of flattening them into a generic missing-limit state.

referenced_resources.description: Operator documentation describing the trade-risk review bundle and dossier workflow.
Operator guide covering trade-risk coverage semantics and no-borrowing posture.
Governed source-catalog manifest available during trade screening review.
Governed reference-values manifest available during trade screening review.
Governed MRL-enforcement manifest available during trade screening review.
Governed legal-authorities manifest available during trade screening review.
Governed jurisdiction-coverage manifest used to interpret exact, partial, anchor-only, and explicit-gap lanes.
Jurisdiction-coverage records for US used during trade screening review.
Jurisdiction-coverage records for CODEX_GLOBAL used during trade screening review.
Jurisdiction-coverage records for CN used during trade screening review.
Substance-scoped reference-value records reviewed alongside the trade-screening result.
Substance-scoped MRL-enforcement records reviewed alongside the trade-screening result.

limitations.message: Trade-risk review bundle supports governed screening review and audit handoff only; it is not a final market-clearance or regulatory acceptance decision.

notes: Bundle packages a governed trade-risk screening result with explicit coverage semantics, review prompts, and pinned review resources.
No-borrowing semantics are preserved: missing jurisdiction coverage remains explicit and is not silently substituted from another authority.
Use this bundle for internal trade-screening review and audit handoff, not as a market-clearance or regulator-acceptance packet.
Trade-risk screening preserves jurisdiction separation and does not borrow MRLs or reference values across authorities.

A pass status only means all applicable governed limits that were actually found were met for that jurisdiction; inspect MRL and reference-value coverage statuses before treating the lane as complete.
Inconclusive jurisdictions remain active review signals, not implicit passes: CN, CODEX_GLOBAL, US.
Review-required status means at least one jurisdiction remains failed, invalid, inconclusive, or coverage-limited under the shipped MRL/reference-value scope.
Illustrative governed trade-risk review bundle for cross-jurisdiction residue screening.

source_governance_snapshot.title: GB 2763-2026 National Food Safety Standard - Maximum Residue Limits for Pesticides in Food
source_governance_snapshot.organization: Agricultural Product Quality and Safety Center, Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Affairs, China
notes: Official published standard text for GB 2763-2026.
The NHC announcement states that the standard took effect on 2026-03-01.

source_governance_snapshot.title: Announcement on GB 2763-2026 National Food Safety Standard - Maximum Residue Limits for Pesticides in Food
source_governance_snapshot.organization: National Health Commission of the People's Republic of China
notes: Official announcement publishing GB 2763-2026.
The announcement states that GB 2763-2026 replaces GB 2763-2021 and GB 2763.1-2022 and took effect on 2026-03-01.

source_governance_snapshot.title: Codex Pesticides Residues in Food Online Database
notes: Official Codex pesticide database used for current CXL lookup.
The public page states that the database includes CXLs up to and including the Forty-seventh CAC Session (November 2024).

source_governance_snapshot.title: FAO Pesticide Registration Toolkit dietary risks overview
notes: Official FAO toolkit summary of dietary pesticide risk assessment principles and the role of JMPR/Codex methods.
Used in Dietary MCP as governed methodology metadata for IEDI and IESTI support context.

source_governance_snapshot.title: Glyphosate (JMPR 2016 evaluation)
notes: Official JMPR evaluation used as governed Codex-global residue and reference-value context for glyphosate.

source_governance_snapshot.title: 40 CFR 180.364 - Glyphosate; tolerances for residues
notes: Official GovInfo section extract used for exact wave-1 glyphosate tolerance values.

source_governance_snapshot.title: 40 CFR Part 180 - Tolerances and Exemptions for Pesticide Chemical Residues in Food (2024 annual edition)
notes: Official GovInfo annual edition of 40 CFR Part 180.
Used as the primary wave-1 U.S. pesticide tolerance legal anchor.

limitations.message: This dossier is version-pinned through release and resource fingerprints but is not cryptographically signed in v0.1.

notes: Version-pinned trade-risk review dossier freezes the exact screening bundle, release metadata hashes, and review documentation fingerprints used during internal screening review.
This dossier preserves explicit no-borrowing semantics and should be used when downstream reviewers need a portable record of why a lane was exact, partial, anchor-only, or an explicit gap.
This dossier supports governed internal review only and is not a final market-clearance, shipment-release, or regulatory acceptance package.
Bundle packages a governed trade-risk screening result with explicit coverage semantics, review prompts, and pinned review resources.
No-borrowing semantics are preserved: missing jurisdiction coverage remains explicit and is not silently substituted from another authority.
Use this bundle for internal trade-screening review and audit handoff, not as a market-clearance or regulator-acceptance packet.
Trade-risk screening preserves jurisdiction separation and does not borrow MRLs or reference values across authorities.

A pass status only means all applicable governed limits that were actually found were met for that jurisdiction; inspect MRL and reference-value coverage statuses before treating the lane as complete.
Inconclusive jurisdictions remain active review signals, not implicit passes: CN, CODEX_GLOBAL, US.
Review-required status means at least one jurisdiction remains failed, invalid, inconclusive, or coverage-limited under the shipped MRL/reference-value scope.
